name: Strait of Hormuz
description: Narrows between Iran and Oman. Handles ~21M bbl/day — roughly 1/3 of all seaborne-traded oil.

name: Suez Canal / SUMED Pipeline
description: Connects Red Sea to Mediterranean. ~5.5M bbl/day of oil and refined products transit through.

name: Bab el-Mandeb Strait
description: Between Yemen and Djibouti/Eritrea. Links Indian Ocean to Red Sea route toward Suez Canal.

name: Strait of Malacca
description: Between Malaysia and Indonesia. ~16M bbl/day. Primary route for Persian Gulf oil to East Asia.

name: Turkish Straits (Bosphorus)
description: Connects Black Sea to Mediterranean. ~2.4M bbl/day. Key route for Russian and Caspian oil exports.

name: Panama Canal
description: Connects Pacific and Atlantic. ~0.9M bbl/day. Mainly U.S. refined product and Latin American crude.

name: Cape of Good Hope
description: Alternative to Suez Canal for supertankers (VLCC). ~6M bbl/day. Longer but no canal size restrictions.
